patch.value: Extension sandbox profiles are advisory-only — no runtime enforcement of declared permission boundaries

extension-policy.ts evaluates sandbox profiles (none/restricted/strict) and produces warnings/blocks based on the manifest `permissions` declaration and `sandbox_profile`. However, the actual extension module is loaded via dynamic import() in the same Node.js process with no VM isolation, permissions API restrictions, or OS-level sandbox. The 'restricted' and 'strict' profiles only block the extension from LOADING if it declares conflicting permissions — they do not prevent a malicious extension from actually spawning processes, writing files, or opening network connections at runtime. An ADR already records this as advisory design. The gap worth tracking: a clear user-facing warning in `pm extension doctor` and `pm health` that sandbox profiles are declaration-based, not runtime-enforced, so users understand the trust model.

- `pm health`/doctor surfaces the advisory-only sandbox trust caveat
- Caveat documented wherever sandbox_profile is described
- No false runtime-enforcement claim (consistent with ADR pm-6ef3)

value.author: sdk-core-agent
value.text: sdk-core-agent starting: one-line advisory-trust-model caveat in pm extension doctor sandbox/permission output; verify pm health makes no runtime-enforcement claim.

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: Trust-model evidence: EXTENSIONS.md and package doctor policy summary now state sandbox_profile/permissions are declaration-based load gates, not runtime isolation.

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: Final verification evidence: pnpm build, pnpm typecheck, pnpm contracts:check, pnpm quality:static, pnpm quality:docs-links, pnpm security:scan, pnpm audit --audit-level low, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (149 files, 2619 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines), pnpm changelog:pm:check, pm health --check-only --summary, pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift, Sentry/telemetry gate (critical=0 high=0 telemetry ok), and isolated temp smoke for command-kit install/echo/list flag/package doctor all passed. Residual validation warning is the known pre-existing 116 older closed items missing resolution fields; history drift is clean.

patch.value: Documented advisory-only sandbox/permissions model and surfaced policy posture in package doctor output.
Operators understand declared sandbox profiles are load-policy metadata, not runtime isolation.
EXTENSIONS docs and package doctor policy summary now state the advisory enforcement caveat; tests passed.
Sandbox profile advisory trust model documented and exposed.
